3	import (
4		"context"
5		"crypto/rand"
6		"crypto/sha256"
7		"encoding/binary"
8		"errors"

10		"sort"

15		"github.com/easel/fizeau/internal/provider/openai"
16		"golang.org/x/sync/singleflight"
17	)
18	
19	// CatalogProbeFunc performs a single /v1/models discovery request against a
20	// specific endpoint. Implementations should return:
21	//   - a ReachabilityError (see internal/provider/openai) when the endpoint
22	//     is unreachable or returns 5xx.
23	//   - a sentinel-wrapped error (errDiscoveryUnsupported) when /v1/models
24	//     returns 404 — the endpoint exists but doesn't expose discovery.
25	//   - a plain error otherwise (auth failures etc. — cache records but
26	//     does not treat as unreachable).
27	//
28	// The ids slice should preserve server-returned order.
29	type CatalogProbeFunc func(ctx context.Context) (ids []string, err error)
30	
31	// Default cache timings. Overridable via catalogCacheOptions.
32	const (
33		defaultCatalogFreshTTL            = 60 * time.Second
34		defaultCatalogStaleTTL            = 10 * time.Minute
35		defaultCatalogUnreachableCooldown = 30 * time.Second
36		defaultCatalogUnreachableJitter   = 5 * time.Second
37		defaultCatalogAsyncRefreshTimeout = 30 * time.Second
38	)
39	
40	// catalogCacheKey identifies the cache entry. Uses fingerprinted auth so
41	// different API keys for the same baseURL don't share cached state.
42	type catalogCacheKey struct {
43		BaseURL     string
44		APIKeyHash  [sha256.Size]byte
45		HeadersHash [sha256.Size]byte
46	}
47	
48	// String returns a deterministic key identity suitable for singleflight.
49	// The hash is already collision-resistant; concatenate the three components
50	// so the singleflight deduplicates by the full identity, not by baseURL alone.
51	func (k catalogCacheKey) String() string {
52		var b strings.Builder
53		b.WriteString(k.BaseURL)
54		b.WriteByte('|')
55		for _, x := range k.APIKeyHash {
56			b.WriteByte(hexDigit(x >> 4))
57			b.WriteByte(hexDigit(x & 0x0f))
58		}
59		b.WriteByte('|')
60		for _, x := range k.HeadersHash {
61			b.WriteByte(hexDigit(x >> 4))
62			b.WriteByte(hexDigit(x & 0x0f))
63		}
64		return b.String()
65	}
66	
67	func hexDigit(b byte) byte {
68		if b < 10 {
69			return '0' + b
70		}
71		return 'a' + b - 10
72	}
73	
74	// catalogEntry is the per-key cached state. Never exposed to callers without
75	// deep-copying, so cache mutation is impossible from the outside.
76	type catalogEntry struct {
77		IDs                []string  // last successful discovery (server order)
78		FetchedAt          time.Time // zero when never fetched
79		LastErr            error     // the most recent error; nil on success
80		UnreachableAt      time.Time // zero unless last attempt produced a ReachabilityError
81		DiscoverySupported bool      // false when /v1/models returns 404 (passthrough mode)
82	}
83	
84	// catalogCache is the service-scope live-catalog cache with stale-while-
85	// revalidate semantics + unreachable cooldown + cold-miss coalescing via
86	// singleflight. One instance per service.
87	type catalogCache struct {
88		mu  sync.Mutex
89		mem map[catalogCacheKey]*catalogEntry
90		sf  singleflight.Group
91		// opts is captured at construction; read without mu (immutable).
92		opts catalogCacheOptions
93	}
94	
95	// catalogCacheOptions controls timings + test hooks. Production uses
96	// defaults via newCatalogCache().
97	type catalogCacheOptions struct {
98		FreshTTL            time.Duration
99		StaleTTL            time.Duration
100		UnreachableCooldown time.Duration
101		UnreachableJitter   time.Duration
102		AsyncRefreshTimeout time.Duration
103		Now                 func() time.Time // injectable for tests
104		RandInt63n          func(n int64) int64
105	}
106	
107	func (o catalogCacheOptions) withDefaults() catalogCacheOptions {
108		if o.FreshTTL <= 0 {
109			o.FreshTTL = defaultCatalogFreshTTL
110		}
111		if o.StaleTTL <= 0 {
112			o.StaleTTL = defaultCatalogStaleTTL
113		}
114		if o.UnreachableCooldown <= 0 {
115			o.UnreachableCooldown = defaultCatalogUnreachableCooldown
116		}
117		// UnreachableJitter may legitimately be 0 (tests); don't override.
118		if o.AsyncRefreshTimeout <= 0 {
119			o.AsyncRefreshTimeout = defaultCatalogAsyncRefreshTimeout
120		}
121		if o.Now == nil {
122			o.Now = time.Now
123		}
124		if o.RandInt63n == nil {
125			o.RandInt63n = cryptoRandInt63n
126		}
127		return o
128	}
129	
130	// cryptoRandInt63n uses crypto/rand for the jitter randomization. Not
131	// performance-critical — at most one call per unreachable miss.
132	func cryptoRandInt63n(n int64) int64 {
133		if n <= 0 {
134			return 0
135		}
136		r, err := rand.Int(rand.Reader, big.NewInt(n))
137		if err != nil {
138			// Vanishingly unlikely; fall back to a non-zero bound so we
139			// never return the exact cooldown and risk synchronized retries
140			// across many goroutines.
141			return int64(time.Now().UnixNano()) % n
142		}
143		return r.Int64()
144	}
145	
146	func newCatalogCache(opts catalogCacheOptions) *catalogCache {
147		return &catalogCache{
148			mem:  make(map[catalogCacheKey]*catalogEntry),
149			opts: opts.withDefaults(),
150		}
151	}
152	
153	// newCatalogCacheKey builds a cache key from endpoint identity. api_key and
154	// headers are hashed so the key is stable but doesn't carry secrets in the
155	// map key's string representation.
156	func newCatalogCacheKey(baseURL, apiKey string, headers map[string]string) catalogCacheKey {
157		return catalogCacheKey{
158			BaseURL:     baseURL,
159			APIKeyHash:  sha256.Sum256([]byte(apiKey)),
160			HeadersHash: hashHeaders(headers),
161		}
162	}
163	
164	// hashHeaders fingerprints a headers map in a deterministic way so ordering
165	// doesn't affect the hash.
166	func hashHeaders(headers map[string]string) [sha256.Size]byte {
167		if len(headers) == 0 {
168			return sha256.Sum256(nil)
169		}
170		keys := make([]string, 0, len(headers))
171		for k := range headers {
172			keys = append(keys, k)
173		}
174		sort.Strings(keys)
175		h := sha256.New()
176		lenBuf := make([]byte, 8)
177		for _, k := range keys {
178			v := headers[k]
179			binary.BigEndian.PutUint64(lenBuf, uint64(len(k)))
180			_, _ = h.Write(lenBuf)
181			_, _ = h.Write([]byte(k))
182			binary.BigEndian.PutUint64(lenBuf, uint64(len(v)))
183			_, _ = h.Write(lenBuf)
184			_, _ = h.Write([]byte(v))
185		}
186		var out [sha256.Size]byte
187		copy(out[:], h.Sum(nil))
188		return out
189	}
190	
191	// CatalogResult is what callers receive from Get. All fields are copies —
192	// mutating them does not affect cache state.
193	type CatalogResult struct {
194		IDs                []string  // server-order model IDs; empty when discovery unsupported or never fetched successfully
195		FetchedAt          time.Time // zero when no successful fetch has occurred
196		DiscoverySupported bool      // false when /v1/models returned 404; callers passthrough
197		LastErr            error     // last probe's error if any; may be a ReachabilityError
198		FromCache          bool      // true when served from cache (fresh or stale)
199		Stale              bool      // true when served from a stale cache entry (async refresh kicked)
200	}
201	
202	// Get looks up the cache and probes if necessary. Returns a CatalogResult
203	// plus an error. Semantics:
204	//
205	//   - Fresh hit: returns cached IDs + no error. FromCache=true, Stale=false.
206	//   - Stale hit: returns cached IDs + kicks background refresh via
207	//     singleflight. FromCache=true, Stale=true.
208	//   - Unreachable within cooldown: returns the cached ReachabilityError.
209	//     FromCache=true. No fresh probe attempted.
210	//   - Cold miss: synchronous probe via singleflight (one probe for N
211	//     concurrent callers). Populates cache with result.
212	//
213	// The CatalogResult is always returned; inspect it even when err is non-nil.
214	func (c *catalogCache) Get(ctx context.Context, key catalogCacheKey, probe CatalogProbeFunc) (CatalogResult, error) {
215		c.mu.Lock()
216		e, ok := c.mem[key]
217		now := c.opts.Now()
218		if ok {
219			// Unreachable cooldown active?
220			if !e.UnreachableAt.IsZero() {
221				cooldown := c.opts.UnreachableCooldown
222				if c.opts.UnreachableJitter > 0 {
223					cooldown += time.Duration(c.opts.RandInt63n(int64(c.opts.UnreachableJitter)))
224				}
225				if now.Sub(e.UnreachableAt) < cooldown {
226					result := snapshotEntry(e)
227					result.FromCache = true
228					c.mu.Unlock()
229					return result, e.LastErr
230				}
231			}
232			// Fresh?
233			if !e.FetchedAt.IsZero() && now.Sub(e.FetchedAt) < c.opts.FreshTTL && e.LastErr == nil {
234				result := snapshotEntry(e)
235				result.FromCache = true
236				c.mu.Unlock()
237				return result, nil
238			}
239			// Stale — within StaleTTL of last successful fetch?
240			if !e.FetchedAt.IsZero() && now.Sub(e.FetchedAt) < c.opts.StaleTTL && e.LastErr == nil {
241				result := snapshotEntry(e)
242				result.FromCache = true
243				result.Stale = true
244				c.mu.Unlock()
245				// Kick async refresh — singleflight coalesces parallel callers.
246				parentCtx := context.WithoutCancel(ctx)
247				go func() {
248					ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(parentCtx, c.opts.AsyncRefreshTimeout)
249					defer cancel()
250					_, _, _ = c.sf.Do(key.String(), func() (interface{}, error) {
251						return c.probe(ctx, key, probe)
252					})
253				}()
254				return result, nil
255			}
256		}
257		c.mu.Unlock()
258	
259		// Cold miss or beyond StaleTTL or previous failure outside cooldown:
260		// synchronous probe via singleflight.
261		r, err, _ := c.sf.Do(key.String(), func() (interface{}, error) {
262			return c.probe(ctx, key, probe)
263		})
264		if r == nil {
265			return CatalogResult{LastErr: err}, err
266		}
267		result := r.(CatalogResult)
268		return result, err
269	}
270	
271	// probe executes the probe, updates the cache, and returns a CatalogResult.
272	// Runs inside singleflight — one call per key even under concurrent misses.
273	func (c *catalogCache) probe(ctx context.Context, key catalogCacheKey, probeFn CatalogProbeFunc) (CatalogResult, error) {
274		ids, err := probeFn(ctx)
275		c.mu.Lock()
276		defer c.mu.Unlock()
277		now := c.opts.Now()
278		e, ok := c.mem[key]
279		if !ok {
280			e = &catalogEntry{DiscoverySupported: true}
281			c.mem[key] = e
282		}
283		e.LastErr = err
284		switch {
285		case err == nil:
286			// Successful probe.
287			e.IDs = append([]string(nil), ids...)
288			e.FetchedAt = now
289			e.UnreachableAt = time.Time{}
290			e.DiscoverySupported = true
291		case isDiscoveryUnsupported(err):
292			// 404 on /v1/models — endpoint exists but doesn't expose discovery.
293			// Fall back to passthrough: callers send their original model string
294			// without normalization.
295			e.DiscoverySupported = false
296			e.UnreachableAt = time.Time{}
297			// IDs intentionally left as-is from prior success (if any); most
298			// likely empty on fresh endpoints.
299		case isReachabilityErr(err):
300			// 5xx or network failure — start cooldown.
301			e.UnreachableAt = now
302		default:
303			// Other errors (auth 401/403, unexpected shape). Record but do NOT
304			// treat as unreachable. Downstream Chat calls may still succeed
305			// if auth works at the chat layer (unusual but possible), or will
306			// surface their own errors.
307			e.UnreachableAt = time.Time{}
308		}
309		return snapshotEntry(e), err
310	}
311	
312	// snapshotEntry copies entry state for safe return outside the lock.
313	func snapshotEntry(e *catalogEntry) CatalogResult {
314		return CatalogResult{
315			IDs:                append([]string(nil), e.IDs...),
316			FetchedAt:          e.FetchedAt,
317			DiscoverySupported: e.DiscoverySupported,
318			LastErr:            e.LastErr,
319		}
320	}
321	
322	// errDiscoveryUnsupported is the sentinel for "endpoint exists but /v1/models
323	// returns 404." CatalogProbeFunc implementations return this so the cache
324	// knows to switch to passthrough.
325	var errDiscoveryUnsupported = &discoveryUnsupportedError{}
326	
327	type discoveryUnsupportedError struct{}
328	
329	func (e *discoveryUnsupportedError) Error() string {
330		return "agent: endpoint does not support /v1/models discovery"
331	}
332	
333	// ErrDiscoveryUnsupported returns the sentinel. Callers compare via
334	// errors.Is(err, ErrDiscoveryUnsupported()).
335	func ErrDiscoveryUnsupported() error { return errDiscoveryUnsupported }
336	
337	func isDiscoveryUnsupported(err error) bool {
338		for e := err; e != nil; {
339			if _, ok := e.(*discoveryUnsupportedError); ok {
340				return true
341			}
342			if u, ok := e.(interface{ Unwrap() error }); ok {
343				e = u.Unwrap()
344				continue
345			}
346			break
347		}
348		return false
349	}
350	
351	// isReachabilityErr reports whether err carries the openai.ErrEndpointUnreachable
352	// sentinel. Probe implementations wrap transport/5xx failures as
353	// *openai.ReachabilityError; this cache uses errors.Is to detect them.
354	func isReachabilityErr(err error) bool {
355		if err == nil {
356			return false
357		}
358		return errors.Is(err, openai.ErrEndpointUnreachable)
359	}

3	import (
4		"context"
5		"errors"
6		"fmt"
7		"sync"
8		"sync/atomic"
9		"testing"
10		"time"
11	
12		"github.com/easel/fizeau/internal/provider/openai"
13		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
14		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
15	)
16	
17	// stubClock is a deterministic time source for tests.
18	type stubClock struct {
19		mu  sync.Mutex
20		now time.Time
21	}
22	
23	func newStubClock() *stubClock {
24		return &stubClock{now: time.Date(2026, 4, 21, 12, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)}
25	}
26	
27	func (c *stubClock) Now() time.Time {
28		c.mu.Lock()
29		defer c.mu.Unlock()
30		return c.now
31	}
32	
33	func (c *stubClock) advance(d time.Duration) {
34		c.mu.Lock()
35		c.now = c.now.Add(d)
36		c.mu.Unlock()
37	}
38	
39	func testCache(clock *stubClock) *catalogCache {
40		return newCatalogCache(catalogCacheOptions{
41			FreshTTL:            10 * time.Second,
42			StaleTTL:            60 * time.Second,
43			UnreachableCooldown: 5 * time.Second,
44			UnreachableJitter:   0, // deterministic tests
45			Now:                 clock.Now,
46			RandInt63n:          func(n int64) int64 { return 0 },
47		})
48	}
49	
50	func testKey(baseURL string) catalogCacheKey {
51		return newCatalogCacheKey(baseURL, "apikey", map[string]string{"X-Test": "yes"})
52	}
53	
54	func TestCatalogCache_FreshHitAvoidsNetwork(t *testing.T) {
55		clock := newStubClock()
56		cache := testCache(clock)
57		key := testKey("http://host/v1")
58	
59		var callCount atomic.Int32
60		probe := func(ctx context.Context) ([]string, error) {
61			callCount.Add(1)
62			return []string{"model-a", "model-b"}, nil
63		}
64	
65		// Cold miss → synchronous probe.
66		r1, err := cache.Get(context.Background(), key, probe)
67		require.NoError(t, err)
68		assert.Equal(t, []string{"model-a", "model-b"}, r1.IDs)
69		assert.False(t, r1.FromCache, "cold miss is not from cache")
70		assert.EqualValues(t, 1, callCount.Load())
71	
72		// Fresh hit within FreshTTL → no probe.
73		clock.advance(5 * time.Second)
74		r2, err := cache.Get(context.Background(), key, probe)
75		require.NoError(t, err)
76		assert.Equal(t, []string{"model-a", "model-b"}, r2.IDs)
77		assert.True(t, r2.FromCache)
78		assert.False(t, r2.Stale)
79		assert.EqualValues(t, 1, callCount.Load(), "fresh hit must not re-probe")
80	}
81	
82	func TestCatalogCache_StaleServesCachedAndAsyncRefreshes(t *testing.T) {
83		clock := newStubClock()
84		cache := testCache(clock)
85		key := testKey("http://host/v1")
86	
87		var callCount atomic.Int32
88		refreshedCh := make(chan struct{}, 2)
89		probe := func(ctx context.Context) ([]string, error) {
90			n := callCount.Add(1)
91			if n > 1 {
92				refreshedCh <- struct{}{}
93			}
94			return []string{"fresh-model"}, nil
95		}
96	
97		// Seed the cache.
98		_, _ = cache.Get(context.Background(), key, probe)
99	
100		// Advance past FreshTTL but within StaleTTL.
101		clock.advance(20 * time.Second)
102	
103		// Stale Get returns cached + kicks async refresh.
104		r, err := cache.Get(context.Background(), key, probe)
105		require.NoError(t, err)
106		assert.True(t, r.FromCache)
107		assert.True(t, r.Stale, "result must be flagged stale")
108		assert.Equal(t, []string{"fresh-model"}, r.IDs)
109	
110		// Wait for async refresh to complete.
111		select {
112		case <-refreshedCh:
113			// refreshed
114		case <-time.After(2 * time.Second):
115			t.Fatal("async refresh did not fire within 2s")
116		}
117	
118		// After refresh, the next call should see the fresh fetch timestamp.
119		r2, err := cache.Get(context.Background(), key, probe)
120		require.NoError(t, err)
121		assert.True(t, r2.FromCache, "still within FreshTTL of the refresh")
122	}
123	
124	func TestCatalogCache_AsyncRefreshUsesConfiguredDeadline(t *testing.T) {
125		clock := newStubClock()
126		cache := newCatalogCache(catalogCacheOptions{
127			FreshTTL:            10 * time.Second,
128			StaleTTL:            60 * time.Second,
129			UnreachableCooldown: 5 * time.Second,
130			UnreachableJitter:   0,
131			AsyncRefreshTimeout: 40 * time.Millisecond,
132			Now:                 clock.Now,
133			RandInt63n:          func(n int64) int64 { return 0 },
134		})
135		key := testKey("http://host/v1")
136	
137		var callCount atomic.Int32
138		deadlineCh := make(chan time.Duration, 1)
139		doneCh := make(chan error, 1)
140		probe := func(ctx context.Context) ([]string, error) {
141			callCount.Add(1)
142			deadline, ok := ctx.Deadline()
143			if !ok {
144				deadlineCh <- -1
145				doneCh <- context.Canceled
146				return nil, context.Canceled
147			}
148			deadlineCh <- time.Until(deadline)
149			<-ctx.Done()
150			err := ctx.Err()
151			doneCh <- err
152			return nil, err
153		}

816	// separate failure mode reserved for a future filter reason.
817	func providerCredentialMissingMap(cfg ServiceConfigSource) map[string]string {
818		if cfg == nil {
819			return nil
820		}
821		names := cfg.ProviderNames()
822		if len(names) == 0 {
823			return nil
824		}
825		provider, ok := cfg.(interface {
826			Provider(name string) (ServiceProviderEntry, bool)
827		})
828		if !ok {
829			return nil
830		}
831		out := make(map[string]string)
832		for _, name := range names {
833			pcfg, ok := provider.Provider(name)
834			if !ok {

836			}
837			location, missing := credentialMissingForProvider(name, pcfg)
838			if missing {

841		}

843			return nil
844		}

846	}

861		location := fmt.Sprintf("providers.%s.api_key (or OPENROUTER_API_KEY env)", name)

18	// ScoredModel is a discovered model with a selection preference score.
19	// Higher scores are preferred by the auto-selection logic.
20	type ScoredModel struct {
21		// ID is the model identifier returned by the server's /v1/models endpoint.
22		ID string
23		// CatalogRef is the catalog target ID if this model is recognized in the
24		// model catalog for the provider's surface. Empty for unrecognized models.
25		CatalogRef string
26		// PatternMatch is true when this model matched the configured model_pattern.
27		PatternMatch bool
28		// Score summarises the selection preference: 3 = catalog-recognized,
29		// 2 = pattern-matched, 1 = uncategorized.
30		Score int
31	}
32	
33	// DiscoverModels queries the generic /v1/models endpoint through the shared
34	// OpenAI-compatible SDK. It is kept here as a package-level compatibility
35	// wrapper for existing provider tests and callers inside this package.
36	func DiscoverModels(ctx context.Context, baseURL, apiKey string) ([]string, error) {
37		return openaicompat.DiscoverModels(ctx, baseURL, apiKey)
38	}
39	
40	// RankModels scores and sorts a list of discovered model IDs by selection
41	// preference:
42	//
43	//   - Score 3 — catalog-recognized: the model ID appears in knownModels (a map
44	//     from concrete model ID to catalog target ID, e.g. from
45	//     Catalog.AllConcreteModels). These are explicitly tracked models; prefer
46	//     them when auto-selecting.
47	//   - Score 2 — pattern-matched: the model ID matches the case-insensitive
48	//     pattern regex (pattern == "" means this tier is skipped).
49	//   - Score 1 — uncategorized: known to the server but not in the catalog or
50	//     pattern.
51	//
52	// Within each score tier, the original server-returned order is preserved.
53	// Returns an error only if pattern is non-empty and fails to compile.
54	func RankModels(candidates []string, knownModels map[string]string, pattern string) ([]ScoredModel, error) {
55		var patternRe *regexp.Regexp
56		if pattern != "" {
57			re, err := regexp.Compile("(?i)" + pattern)
58			if err != nil {
59				return nil, fmt.Errorf("discovery: invalid model_pattern %q: %w", pattern, err)
60			}
61			patternRe = re
62		}
63	
64		scored := make([]ScoredModel, 0, len(candidates))
65		for _, id := range candidates {
66			sm := ScoredModel{ID: id, Score: 1}
67			if ref, ok := knownModels[id]; ok {
68				sm.CatalogRef = ref
69				sm.Score = 3
70			} else if patternRe != nil && patternRe.MatchString(id) {
71				sm.PatternMatch = true
72				sm.Score = 2
73			}
74			scored = append(scored, sm)
75		}
76	
77		// Stable sort: higher score first, original order preserved within tier.
78		sort.SliceStable(scored, func(i, j int) bool {
79			return scored[i].Score > scored[j].Score
80		})
81		return scored, nil
82	}
83	
84	// SelectModel picks the preferred model ID from a ranked list. Returns ""
85	// if the list is empty.
86	func SelectModel(ranked []ScoredModel) string {
87		if len(ranked) == 0 {
88			return ""
89		}
90		return ranked[0].ID
91	}
